item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a fun article, but I'd like a little more backstory on the bees themselves. They seem to come from nowhere. It doesn't really make sense for this to happen with all honey bees, or it would have manifested more times. I do like the warning shot, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find it hard to believe that the Foundation would allow a researcher to interview a guy possesed by aggresive bees without even asking him to use to some kind of bee suit. But I guess that would deny the last funny thing in the skip, so I'll let it pass. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A little late, but somebody has to say it.

What if your brain was bees?

item.title: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
edit: edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show proposed edits
- Hide

To link to other Bee related SCPs, I propose the following crosslinks:

The brain of an SCP-2324-A instance is replaced with a new structure superficially resembling a beehive

to

The brain of an SCP-2324-A instance is replaced with a new structure superficially resembling a beehive1

and

SCP-2324-A instances are capable of speech in the host's native language, and an instance will refer to itself as an individual warship belonging to a greater army known by such names as “the Hive Authority,” “Her Majesty’s Fleet,” and “the Queen’s Navy.”

to

SCP-2324-A instances are capable of speech in the host's native language, and an instance will refer to itself as an individual warship belonging to a greater army known by such names as “the Hive Authority,” “Her Majesty’s Fleet,” and “the Queen’s Navy.”2

Footnotes
1. Resembling SCP-2401 infection.
2. Investigation into possible links to SCP-2491 is ongoing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I can't deny that this is amusing, and that it succeeds in creating a pretty interesting narrative, this just feels… incomplete I guess? I don't know, just the whole concept of "a phenomenon ascociated with the western honeybee and its parasitic interaction with humans and is also keter" is just something that feels like wasted potential when put in such a shortform style.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: no moustaches; they're all female.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article is really good, and just serious enough to not be a -J. Also, it reminds me of SCP-400 in a way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted. I can't believe the Foundation would be so incompetent as to not have any sort of protective clothing or barrier between the infected and the interviewing doctor.

Beyond that, though, I really like this article. I wish I could upvote it, but that really breaks it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I find the battleship bit amusing, but I find the actual interview to be rather unrealistic. After all, the SCP is essentially a person full of bees. The Foundation was surely aware of this before the interview - and yet the interview occurred without any protective or preventative measures for the interviewer.

While the amusement is there, the rest of the writing/handling is just too clunky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Pain… The Pain… THE PAIN!

+1 (barely) because it made me smile, though the goofiness of it sort of feels out of place? Maybe it would be better served as a -J! Just my two cents.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *clears throat* Evidence

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It wasn't a direct reference, but I was a bit inspired by Bioshock!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know I'm a little late to the party, but…

Pores in SCP-2324-A-1's left arm open, unleashing a small swarm of honey bees

Is this a BioShock reference? Because if it is… heck, I'll upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea of humans becoming battleships. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really overdone concept, but the humour made it work for me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Part of me wants to see 2401 and 2324 go to war with each other.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Comparing this to SCP-2401, I actually think I like this one more. It raises a lot more unsettling questions when you read it in a serious mindset… makes me think of some of the other biotech skips. Infiltration units gone stupid without central command, perhaps?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's like you tried to write the skip Bees is based off of, not to mention the interview is ridiculous and why would you give a potentially infected personnel access to anything?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He's technically Daryl, but yeah. Depending on personal PoV, he's either the best thing about it or the only reason to watch the show. We'll just call it a happy coincidence (though, for future reference and any WD fans, I am totally imagining Norman Reedus delivering these lines).
http://d.ibtimes.co.uk/en/full/1431060/walking-dead.jpg for reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, a zombie aircraft carrier for sapient bees, which I am totally imagining with big moustaches and British accents?

This is hilarious. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Might work better as a -J. This makes me think too much of the dog with bees in his mouth, so that when he barks he shoots bees at you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …crap, I don't watch that show, is there a character named Darrel Dixon? But no.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am mildly charmed by the quirky semi-absurdist tone this article took. True that there are other SCPs with similar content, but they're distinct enough that it is plausible for them to co-exist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Narrow upvote, charm carried it. Have to ask, is 'Darrel Dixon' a deliberate Walking Dead reference?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, especially at the end where he changed his username, but why on Earth would they give him access to "Foundation-critical matters" while awaiting to see if he has been infected with something that will take over his brain? Especially when it seems to be a hostile Keter?

I still like where he changed his name, but it really is kind of odd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for that!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enemy vessel off the starboard bow! Man the drone turrets! FOR THE QUEEEEEEEEEEN Sorry you feel that way :c

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yet another samey, rehashed article barely skirting by thanks to being mildly funny. Can't say I'm fond of this trend.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You should be fine. As long as articles are different enough to stand out on their own (can't comment on the contents of this), you should be good. We have a green soap statue that kills people when it's not looked at yet that's doing well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …oh shit. I forgot this article existed. Is this something I could get in trouble for for plagiarism? Wasn't my intention. D:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty similar to SCP-2401, another recent addition. This one is more quirky whereas 2401 is dark/body horror, though. I'm not especially enamored with this one, but am sitting at neutral rather than downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was underwhelmed until I reached the end of the description, and thoroughly enjoyed the rest. It's, as MacLeod said, not fantastic, but very entertaining and that's more than enough for an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not fantastic, and it does things that some other articles do better, but dammit, Dave, your particular brand of goofy gets me every time.
